title: The Magicians of Caprona - Title Page (Fragment 1)
text:
The Magicians of Caprona

D iana W ynne J ones

THE MAGICIANS OF CAPRONA

A G REENWILLOW B OOK

vocabulary.definition: A word that describes a noun or pronoun.
vocabulary.example: Blue and tall are adjectives.

vocabulary.definition: Very surprising or impressive.
vocabulary.example: The show had some astonishing special effects.

vocabulary.definition: A person who writes books or other texts.
vocabulary.example: The author of the book is well-known.

vocabulary.definition: To keep away from or refrain from doing something.
vocabulary.example: Avoid making mistakes by preparing well.

vocabulary.definition: A set of written, printed, or blank pages fastened together within covers.
vocabulary.example: She read a fascinating book last night.

vocabulary.definition: A fictional place name, central to the story.
vocabulary.example: The story is set in the city of Caprona.

vocabulary.definition: Of greatest importance; at the centre.
vocabulary.example: Caprona is the central location in the story.

vocabulary.definition: A habitual combination of words often used together.
vocabulary.example: Make a decision is a common collocation.

vocabulary.definition: Related to the context in which something occurs.
vocabulary.example: Understanding words in their contextual meaning is crucial.

vocabulary.definition: A word or phrase conveying an idea or feeling.
vocabulary.example: Idiomatic expressions are common in English.

vocabulary.definition: Relating to fiction; invented story rather than true.
vocabulary.example: The novel features a fictional character living in medieval times.

vocabulary.definition: Name of a publisher.
vocabulary.example: The book was published by Greenwillow Books.

vocabulary.definition: Of great significance or value.
vocabulary.example: This meeting is very important for our project.

vocabulary.definition: Relating to the words or vocabulary of a language.
vocabulary.example: A lexical analysis helps identify key words.

vocabulary.definition: A person who uses magic or has magical powers.
vocabulary.example: The magician performed astonishing tricks at the show.

vocabulary.definition: A spoken or written account of connected events; a story.
vocabulary.example: The narrative is told from the protagonist’s perspective.

vocabulary.definition: To carry out an action or behaviour, to entertain an audience.
vocabulary.example: The magician performed an incredible illusion.

vocabulary.definition: Something that is regarded as more important than others.
vocabulary.example: Safety is a top priority in this project.

vocabulary.definition: An outline or description of features or characteristics.
vocabulary.example: The profile of the vocabulary shows advanced words.

vocabulary.definition: The action or process of choosing something.
vocabulary.example: The selection of words was careful and deliberate.

vocabulary.definition: Sufficiently great or important.
vocabulary.example: The change had significant effects on the economy.

vocabulary.definition: An account of imaginary or real events.
vocabulary.example: The story captivated all the children.

vocabulary.definition: The name of a book, film, or other work.
vocabulary.example: The title of the novel caught my attention.

vocabulary.definition: A word used to describe an action, state, or occurrence.
vocabulary.example: Run, jump, and think are all verbs.
